results.title: tina giant hand peels car baseline
results.prompt: Create a photoreal cinematic candy-commercial styleframe that answers the director's literal question: what if a giant hand peels open Tina's car? Preserve the same 18-year-old Tina, casual wardrobe, pink Lychee Peelerz candy and package, rideshare car, and ordinary sunny neighborhood from the references. Use a wide three-quarter exterior view. One enormous human hand reaches down from outside the frame, pinches a clearly visible seam along the car roof, and peels the car's outer body backward in one flexible sheet like skin from a fruit. Tina stays seated and clearly visible inside, calmly holding the freshly bitten pink candy. Make the giant hand, the pinched seam, and the single curling sheet instantly readable. Playful scale, not threatening or grotesque. This is a simple baseline: one event, no second transformation, no rainbow, no candyland, no magical particles, no extra characters, no text, no watermark.

results.title: tina gummy skin becomes canopy mutation
results.prompt: Create a photoreal cinematic candy-commercial styleframe that tests one continuous-material mutation of the Tina sequence. Preserve Tina's face, age, wardrobe, pink Lychee Peelerz candy and package, car, and grounded neighborhood realism from the references. The car's outer skin has stretched into ONE translucent pink gummy membrane: it is still visibly attached to the peeled-open car below, rises as one broad curling strip, and spreads overhead into a soft canopy carrying Tina upward. Tina grips the attached gummy sheet with one hand and holds the bitten candy with the other. The viewer must be able to trace one uninterrupted material from car body to peel strip to canopy. It should feel elastic and fruit-like, not nylon or cloth. One physical rule only. No rainbow parachute, no candyland, no floating candy shower, no unrelated effects, no text, no watermark.

results.title: raul multi flavor candy mecha baseline

Raul's real face remains visible at the center; Meg watches from the bleachers holding her package. No likeness to any existing robot franchise, no logos beyond Peelerz packaging, no weapons, no candyland, no text, no watermark.
results.text: This commercial styleframe accurately depicts the original multi-flavor mecha transformation request on the bleachers. The mecha follows the specific color-flavor modularity (purple arm, green arm, mango legs), and the translucent candy material with peel-strip connectors is well-rendered. Meg is correctly placed with the packaging, and the composition avoids toy clutter for a premium look. I will show the image to the user.

results.title: giant raul eats small raul baseline

Preserve Raul and Meg's faces, age, wardrobe, mango Peelerz candy and packaging from the references, on high-school football bleachers. Meg looks up, dryly unimpressed. Make both scales and the identity match unmistakable in one frame. The tone is mischievous and impossible, never horror: no teeth close-up, no injury, no distress, no gore. No extra magic, no candyland, no text, no watermark.

results.title: couple anchored two world handoff baseline
results.prompt: Create a cinematic commercial styleframe testing a clean world-transition grammar. Preserve Liz and John's faces, teen age, wardrobe, relative positions, Peelerz candies and packages from the campus reference. Keep the couple centered and physically continuous while the camera makes a circular move around them. Catch the exact handoff frame: the left half remains the ordinary live-action college quad, while a crisp moving boundary passing behind and between them converts the right half into a flat hand-painted animated fruit grove. Their bodies and eyeline remain stable across the seam, as if the worlds change but the couple does not. Use the second reference ONLY for the formal idea of centered people anchored during a medium transition; do not copy its characters, costumes, franchise imagery, or graphic style. One clean boundary, two legible worlds, no portal ring, no collage clutter, no text, no watermark.
